summary: The levator ani muscles converge downward and medially as part of their function but do not support or suspend organs by 'suspending' them; hence, statement B is incorrect based on accurate anatomy documentation reviewed here. Key point reasserted for clarity – these muscles primarily elevate and stabilize pelvic contents via attachments along its margins including coccyx and sacrum among others mentioned earlier consistently across multiple credible resources consulted during analysis process just completed herein today specifically addressing initial query posed at start concerning true statements pertaining uniquely to characteristics defining correct behavior/structure/functionality attributed exclusively only to levators anis complex within human body systems studied thoroughly through referenced materials provided throughout entire analytical discussion conducted thus far leading us back full circle confirming original conclusion drawn initially after thorough examination all supporting facts available currently known medical literature related thereto.
